text.title: Client Control ID Mapping — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Client Control ID Mapping must enforce.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only to a single instance of its target decision; never batch multiple unrelated decisions through one pass.
rule.rationale: Batching collapses traceability and reduces each instance to a debate item.

rule.statement: Every input field carries a declared type and a source path or URL; free-form prose without source is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped inputs let LLMs hallucinate plausible-but-wrong values; typed + sourced inputs make outputs falsifiable.

rule.statement: The output names a single accountable owner (handle, email, or org role with rotation). No 'team', 'we', or 'us'.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership equals no ownership; future readers need a named human to chase when the artefact is stale.

rule.statement: Output carries semver-style version + last_reviewed date; records older than 90 days without review are flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Methodology outputs decay; treating them as immutable is a defect.

rule.statement: Define the automated detector (lint rule, test name, metric query) BEFORE writing the corrective action.
rule.rationale: Writing the fix first leaves rules with no detector; the detector enforces the rule going forward.
